1st Earl of Arran

He was the only son of James Hamilton, 1st Lord Hamilton, and his wife, Mary Stewart, Countess of Arran. Mary was a daughter of King James II of Scotland and his Queen consort Mary …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lanark於1475年4月25日出生在英國蘇格蘭藍納可爾漢密爾頓時，他的父親James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton59歲，母親Mary Stewart of Scotland21歲。他於1503年與Isabel Hamilton在英國蘇格蘭藍納可爾布拉可武德結婚。他於1502年登記服兵役。他於1517年在英國蘇格蘭被列為lord of regency。他去世於1529年3月26日在英國蘇格蘭韋斯特 洛迪安享年53歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭藍納可爾漢密爾頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lanark于1475年4月25日出生在英国苏格兰藍納可爾漢密爾頓时，他的父亲James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton59岁，母亲Mary Stewart of Scotland21岁。他于1503年与Isabel Hamilton在英国苏格兰藍納可爾布拉可武德结婚。他于1502年登记服兵役。他于1517年在英国苏格兰被列为lord of regency。他去世于1529年3月26日在英国苏格兰韋斯特 洛迪安享年53岁，埋葬在苏格兰藍納可爾漢密爾頓。

nameMeanings.name: Hamilton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: habitational name from what is now a deserted village in the parish of Barkby, Leicestershire. This is named from Old English hamel ‘crooked’ + dūn ‘hill’. Hamilton near Glasgow was founded by the English Hamiltons and named after them, and later bearers took their surname from the Scottish place. In the north of Ireland, where many Hamiltons settled in the 17th century, this surname may sometimes have been adopted for Hamill . It has also been adopted as an Anglicized form of southern Irish Ó hUrmholtaigh.

History: This name is borne by one of the most distinguished families of the Scottish nobility; they hold many titles, including marquessate and dukedom of Hamilton, the marquessate of Douglas, the dukedom of Abercorn, and the earldom of Haddington. They are descended from Walter FitzGilbert de Hameldone, a Norman baron who gave his support to Robert the Bruce in the 13th century. A member of this family was Sir William Hamilton (1730–1803), a British diplomat and archaeologist, whose wife, Lady Emma Hamilton (c. 1765–1815), became the mistress of Admiral Horatio Nelson. A branch of the family was established in Ireland by Sir Frederick Hamilton (died 1646), who served in the Swedish army of Gustavus Adolphus. He later became governor of Ulster, and his descendants were created viscounts Boyne. The family have given their name to Newtownhamilton and Hamiltonsbaron in County Armagh. Another branch of the family were to be found in Denmark, where Henrik Albertsen Hamilton (1588–1648) was a noted Latin poet. A 17th-century example of a Hamilton from Glasgow, Scotland, is recorded in the Netherlands, where the name is found in the form Hamelton. Another Scottish Hamilton, James, went to the West Indies in the 18th century and was the father of Alexander Hamilton (1755–1804), who arrived in NY in 1772 and became the first US secretary of the Treasury.

variants.name: Hamill
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire): of Norman origin, a habitational name from Haineville or Henneville in Manche, France, named from the ancient Germanic personal name Hagano + Old French ville ‘settlement’.
English (Lancashire): nickname for a maimed person or someone with a distinctive scar, from Middle English, Old English hamel ‘mutilated, scarred’.
Irish (Ulster): according to MacLysaght, a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hÁdhmaill ‘descendant of Ádhmall’, which he derives from ádhmall ‘active’.

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

nameMeanings.name: Knight,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status or occupational name from Middle English knight ‘retainer, attendant’ (Old English cniht ‘boy, youth, lad)’. The specialized feudal sense ‘a high-ranking tenant bound to serve his lord as a mounted soldier’ is not known to have ever given rise to the surname, although it is not out of the question that it may occasionally have been used as a nickname, perhaps for someone who played the part of an armed knight in a local pageant.
Irish: sometimes adopted for Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the knight’. See also McKnight .

variants.name: Knights
meanings: English (East Anglia): variant of Knight , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McKnight
meanings: Irish: part translation of Gaelic Mac an Ridire ‘son of the horseman (Gaelic ridire)’, formerly Anglicized McRuddery.
Scottish (Dumfriesshire and Galloway) and Irish (Ulster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Neachtain, which is usually Anglicized as McNaughton .

nameMeanings.name: High
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:
nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hay 3.
topographic name for someone who lived ‘(on the) height or hill’, from Middle English heigh ‘height, hilltop’ (Old English hēah). Compare Hight .

variants.name: Hay
meanings: Scottish and English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy called La Haie or La Haye (Old French haie ‘hedge, enclosure’, ‘forest for hunting deer and other animals’, a borrowing of the ancient Germanic word haga). Robert de Haia or de la Haye is known to have come from La Haye-du-Puits in Manche; he was the founder of Boxgrove Priory in Sussex (1123), and holder of the Honor of Halnaker (Sussex) and (by marriage) the barony of Kolswein (Lincolnshire). The Norman name was also taken to Ireland, where it has since flourished in the county of Wexford as Hay and Hayes . Elsewhere in Ireland the name usually has a native Irish origin, see below.

English: topographic name from Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg) or ‘forest fenced off for hunting’ (Old French haie); or else a habitational name from a place so called, such as Hay (in Herefordshire and Westmorland) or Hey in Scammonden (Yorkshire). It was no doubt sometimes synonymous with Hayward .
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah).

variants.name: Hy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heng, Hong, Sang, Dong, Khin, Kin, Kong, Man, Sin, Yong.  Vietnamese Soi, Cong, Dang, Dau, Duoc, Mui, Nhi, Phu, Quyen, Tau.
English: variant of   High  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 解, based on its pronunciation in Taishanese, a variant of Cantonese, see   Xie   2.

nameMeanings.name: Sheriff
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name or nickname from Middle English shirreve, sher(r)eve, sheref(fe) ‘sheriff, officer of the Crown’ (Old English scīrgerēfa, from scīr ‘shire, county’ + (ge)rēfa ‘reeve’), literally the reeve (i.e. steward) of a shire. Compare Reeve .
Muslim (mainly Nigeria, Sierra Leone, and Liberia): variant of   Sharif  . In Sierra Leone and Liberia this name is found mainly among the Mandinka people. Compare   Sirleaf  .

variants.name: Sirleaf
meanings: West African (Liberia): altered form of the predominantly Mandinka Muslim name   Sheriff  .

variants.name: Reeve
meanings: English (East Anglia): occupational name from Middle English reve, refe ‘reeve’ (Old English gerēfa). In medieval England a reeve was an official responsible for the administration of a manor, including organizing work done by the peasants on the land for their lord, collecting rents, selling produce, and so on.
Irish: variant of   Reeves   5.

variants.name: Shreve
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of   Sheriff  . Compare   Shreeve  ,   Shreves  .

variants.name: Sherriff
meanings: English: variant of   Sheriff  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Lanark
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Italian   Lamarca  .

variants.name: Lamarca
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Gino, Salvatore, Carmelo, Cataldo, Pasquale, Sal, Aldo, Carlo, Carmine, Cosimo, Domenic.
Italian (La Marca); Aragonese and Catalan: variant of Marca , with the definite article la.

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: equivalent of George .

variants.name: George
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Lorraine   or variant spelling of   Laurie .

variants.name: Lorraine
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin denoting a migrant from the province of Lorraine in eastern France. This derives its name from Latin Lotharingia ‘territory of the people of Lothar’. The latter is a Germanic personal name derived from hlud ‘fame’ + heri, hari ‘army’. Lorraine began to be used as a girl's name in Scotland in the 19th century, and for a time in the second half of the 20th century enjoyed great popularity, which has since waned.

nameMeanings.name: Admiral
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman:  See  Damerell  .

variants.name: Damerell
meanings: Norman: locative name from Aumale in Seine-Maritime, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lanark was born on 25 April 1475, in Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton, was 59 and his mother, Mary Stewart of Scotland, was 21. He married Isabel Hamilton in 1503, in Blackwood, Lanarkshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. He registered for military service in 1502. In 1517, at the age of 42, his occupation is listed as lord of regency in Scotland, United Kingdom. He died on 26 March 1529, in West Lothian, Scotland, United Kingdom, at the age of 53, and was buried in Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lanark est né le 25 avril 1475, à Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton, avait 59 ans et sa mère, Mary Stewart of Scotland, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Isabel Hamilton en 1503, à Blackwood, Lanarkshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Il s'est inscrite au service militaire en 1502. En 1517, à l’âge de 42 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est lord of regency à Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé le 26 mars 1529, à West Lothian, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 53 ans, et a été enterré à Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lanark am 25. April 1475 in Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton 59 und seine Mutter Mary Stewart of Scotland 21. Er hat 1503 in Blackwood, Lanarkshire, Schottland, United Kingdom Isabel Hamilton geheiratet. Er hat sich 1502 zum Militärdienst angemeldet. 1517, als er 42 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als lord of regency in Schottland, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb am 26. März 1529 im Alter von 53 Jahren in West Lothian, Schottland, United Kingdom und wurde in Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lanark è nato il il 25 aprile 1475 nel Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton, aveva 59 anni e il suo madre, Mary Stewart of Scotland, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Isabel Hamilton nel 1503, nel Blackwood, Lanarkshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Si è arruolato nell'esercito nel 1502. Nel 1517, all'età di 42 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come lord of regency nel Scozia, Regno Unito. Morì il 26 marzo 1529, nel West Lothian, Scozia, Regno Unito, all'età di 53 anni, e fu sepolto a Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lanarkが1475年4月25日にイギリススコットランドラナルクシュアイアハミルトンで生まれた時，父James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamiltonは59歳，母Mary Stewart of Scotlandは21歳であった。1503年にイギリススコットランドラナルクシュアイアブラックウッドでIsabel Hamiltonと結婚した。彼は1502年に兵役登録をした。1517年，42歳の時，イギリススコットランドでの職業はlord of regencyと記録されている。彼は1529年3月26日にイギリススコットランドウエスト ロスアンで53歳で死去し，スコットランドラナルクシュアイアハミルトンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lanark(이)가 1475년 4월 25일 영국 스코틀랜드 랜아르크실르 함이턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton(은)는 59세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Stewart of Scotland(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1503년 영국 스코틀랜드 랜아르크실르 블랙우드에서 Isabel Hamilton(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1502년에 군복무를 위해 등록했다. 1517년, 42세의 나이로 그의 직업은 영국 스코틀랜드에서 lord of regency이었다. 그는 1529년 3월 26일 영국 스코틀랜드 웨스트 로스이언에서 53세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 랜아르크실르 함이턴에 묻혔다.

pt.25 April 1475–26 March 1529: 25 de abril de 1475–26 de março de 1529
pt.St Salvator's Chapel, St Andrew's, Scotl: St. Andrews, Midlothian, Escócia, United Kingdom
pt.Island Arran, Ayreshire, Scotland: Arran, North Ayrshire, Escócia, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lanark nasceu em 25 de abril de 1475, em Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton, tinha 59 anos e sua mãe, Mary Stewart of Scotland, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Isabel Hamilton em 1503, em Blackwood, Lanarkshire, Escócia, United Kingdom. Ele alistou-se no serviço militar em 1502. Em 1517, com 42 anos, sua profissão aparece como lord of regency em Escócia, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 26 de março de 1529, em West Lothian, Escócia, United Kingdom, com 53 anos, e foi sepultado em Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Scotland.

ro.Island Arran, Ayreshire, Scotland: Arran, North Ayrshire, Scoția, United Kingdom

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lanark родился 25 апреля 1475 г. в местности хамилтон, ланаркшир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton было 59 лет, а матери, которую звали Mary Stewart of Scotland, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Isabel Hamilton в 1503 г. в местности блэкквуд, ланаркшир, Шотландия, Великобритания. В 1502 году он поступил на военную службу. В 1517 году, в возрасте 42 лет, работал по профессии lord of regency в местности Шотландия, Великобритания. Он умер 26 марта 1529 г. в местности вест лотян, Шотландия, Великобритания в возрасте 53 лет и был похоронен в местности хамилтон, ланаркшир, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Lord Admiral James Hamilton III Knight, High Sheriff of Lanark nació el 25 de abril de 1475, en Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, James Hamilton -1st Lord Hamilton, tenía 59 años y su mamá, Mary Stewart of Scotland, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Isabel Hamilton en 1503, en Blackwood, Lanarkshire, Escocia, Reino Unido. Se enlistó en el ejército en 1502. En 1517, a la edad de 42 años, su ocupación registrada era lord of regency en Escocia, Reino Unido. Murió el 26 de marzo de 1529, en West Lothian, Escocia, Reino Unido, a la edad de 53 años, y fue sepultado en Hamilton, Lanarkshire, Scotland.
